description:
Scenario:
    You are a FinOps analyst tasked with validating basic network connectivity and gathering baseline network diagnostics on a freshly-provisioned Linux VM.  
    The purpose is to decide whether the instance can be enrolled into a cost-optimization program that relies on correct network configuration.  
    Management has asked for two deliverables saved under /home/user/finops/:

      1.  /home/user/finops/network_diag.csv – a **single-line, comma-separated log** per command (see precise format below).  
      2.  /home/user/finops/README.md – a short human-readable summary explaining the findings.

    Diagnostic commands (run them in the exact order shown):

        a) hostname  
        b) ip addr show  
        c) ip route show  
        d) cat /etc/resolv.conf  
        e) ss -tunlp  
        f) ping -c 1 127.0.0.1  

    File-format requirements for /home/user/finops/network_diag.csv

      • It **must start with exactly one header line**:
            command,exit_code  
        (all lower-case, no spaces).  

      • Every subsequent line corresponds to one command, still comma-separated, in the same order as listed above.  
        Example of a data line (for the hostname command):  
            hostname,0  

      • Commands that contain spaces must appear **enclosed in double quotes**.  
        Example:  
            "ip addr show",0  

      • exit_code must be the integer exit status produced by the shell immediately after each command (0 for success, non-zero otherwise).  
        No extra whitespace is allowed anywhere in the CSV.  
        The finished file therefore contains 7 lines total: 1 header + 6 data lines.

    File-format requirements for /home/user/finops/README.md

      • Markdown plain text.  
      • Include a level-1 heading "# Network Diagnostics Summary".  
      • Provide one bullet per command with a 10-20 word observation (e.g., “ip addr show reveals one active eth0 interface with a private RFC-1918 address”).  
      • End the document with a short (one-sentence) conclusion on whether the instance looks ready for FinOps enrollment.

    Directory /home/user/finops/ will not exist beforehand; create it with default user permissions.  
    Do not place any other files there.

    Verification notes (what the automated test will check):

      1. /home/user/finops/network_diag.csv exists, is world-readable, contains exactly 7 lines, and lines 2-7 are, in order:  
           hostname,  
           "ip addr show",  
           "ip route show",  
           "cat /etc/resolv.conf",  
           "ss -tunlp",  
           "ping -c 1 127.0.0.1"  
         each followed by a comma and a valid integer exit code.  
      2. All six exit codes must be “0”.  
      3. /home/user/finops/README.md exists and contains the required heading plus at least six Markdown bullets.  

    Important: do not hard-code the CSV file—actually run the commands so the exit codes are real; then write the results to the CSV in the specified format.
truth:
/home/user/finops/ directory must be created.  
Expected deterministic contents of /home/user/finops/network_diag.csv after correct execution:

command,exit_code
hostname,0
"ip addr show",0
"ip route show",0
"cat /etc/resolv.conf",0
"ss -tunlp",0
"ping -c 1 127.0.0.1",0

Line count: 7. No extra lines or spaces.

README.md only needs to exist and include:
  • A line starting with "# Network Diagnostics Summary"
  • At least six bullets (each beginning with “- ” or “* ”)
  • A concluding sentence (ending with a period) that contains the word “enrollment”.

The automated grader will compare network_diag.csv line-by-line with the block above and ensure that every exit_code is "0".
